---
title: ""
---

# Beining Liu {#home-title}

---

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="42%"}
![](images/profile.jpg){#profile-photo}
:::

::: {.column width="58%"}

Welcome!

I am a master's student in Quantitative Economics at New York University Stern School of Business. My research interests lie in economic history, political economy, and applied microeconomics. I am particularly interested in how historical shocks, informal institutions, and state-society interactions shape long-run economic and political outcomes.

My current work focuses on China, Japan, and the United States, with projects on the Boxer Rebellion, the Great Depression and entry into scientific careers, and literacy and political change in Tokugawa Japan.

You can find my CV [here](files/CV.pdf).

Contact:

[beining.liu@nyu.edu](mailto:beining.liu@nyu.edu)

GitHub: [BeiningLiu](https://github.com/BeiningLiu)

Google Scholar: to be added

News: I am currently developing projects on historical memory and nationalism in China, the Great Depression and scientific careers in the United States, and literacy and political change in Tokugawa Japan.

:::
::::
